MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Mar-19-2025 Continued To Oct-27-25 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal Msj Materials
Off Calendar Mar-20-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Atty. SHOSHANA KAISER, Esq. (via Zoom videoconference) of Brown Rudnick LLP (tel: 949-752-7100) for Plaintiff.
Atty. BAKARI ZIEGLER, Esq. of Morgan, Lewis & Bockius LLP (tel: 415-442-1173) for Defendants.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANTS ORACLE CORPORATION, AND FACQUISITION CORP.'s Motion To Seal Msj Materials.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling with modifications.

Defendant's motion to seal portions of the moving, opposing, and reply papers related to plaintiff's motion for summary judgment or alternatively, summary adjudication, is granted.

A court can seal a record only if: "(1) There exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the record; (2) The overriding interest supports sealing the record; (3) A substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; (4) The proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and (5) No less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest." (CRC 2.550(d).)

Here, there is an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access. The enforcement of a binding contractual obligation not to disclose serves such an interest. (NBC Subsidiary (KNBC-TV), Inc. v. Superior Court (1999) 20 Cal.4th 1178, 1222, fn. 46.) In addition, the protection of confidential business and financial information constitutes an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access. Here, the Equity Purchase Agreement, Escrow Agreement, and third-party Statement of Work ("SOW"), which incorporates the third-party's Security Requirements, contain confidentiality clauses. (MSJ Compendium Ex. 1, section 5.4; Ex. 2, section 12(h).) Those instruments also contain confidential financial information. A substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced without the sealing order. Disclosure of the confidential information may adversely affect defendant's competitive abilities and perhaps subject defendant to litigation with the third-party.

The proposed redactions are narrowly tailored. Unlike the prior motions to seal, defendant's application is narrow and no longer seeks to seal garden variety notice, indemnity, and choice of law provisions. Lastly, there are no less restrictive means to achieve the overriding interest.

The fact that plaintiff revealed some of the financial information in the complaint does not necessarily bar the court from sealing the adduced documents.

Defendant shall prepare a proposed order setting out the above verbatim and specifically listing the documents to be sealed pursuant to this order.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jan-09-2025 Continued To May-12-25 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal Evolution Capital Partners Iii, Llc'S Motion For Summary Judgement And Documents Lodged Therewith
Off Calendar Feb-07-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANTS ORACLE CORPORATION, AND FACQUISITION CORP.'s Motion To Seal Evolution Capital Partners Iii, Llc's Motion For Summary Judgement And Documents Lodged Therewith.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's motion to seal is denied without prejudice. A court can seal a record only if: "(1) There exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the record; (2) The overriding interest supports sealing the record; (3) A substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; (4) The proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and (5) No less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest." (Rule of Court 2.550(d).) Defendant fails to make that showing.

Defendant argues that its contractual obligation not to disclose and confidential business information constitute an overriding interest to warrant sealing. But "[t]he second element of the overriding interest analysis is there must be a substantial probability that it will be prejudiced absent closure or sealing." (Universal City Studios, Inc. v. Superior Court, Inc. (2003) 110 Cal.App.4th 1273, 1283.) Here, there is no explanation how defendant will be prejudiced if the garden variety notice, indemnity, and choice of law provisions are disclosed. The motion is also not narrowly tailored. For example, it is unclear how plaintiff's mere assertion at UMF 13 that "[t]he Oracle Defendants concede that the Escrow Agreement and the Purchase Agreement are unambiguous" needs to be sealed.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling with a cover letter reflecting compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's motion to seal is denied without prejudice. A court can seal a record only if: "(1) There exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the record; (2) The overriding interest supports sealing the record; (3) A substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; (4) The proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and (5) No less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest." (Rule of Court 2.550(d).) Defendant fails to make that showing.

Defendant argues that its contractual obligation not to disclose and confidential business information constitute an overriding interest to warrant sealing. But "[t]he second element of the overriding interest analysis is there must be a substantial probability that it will be prejudiced absent closure or sealing." (Universal City Studios, Inc. v. Superior Court, Inc. (2003) 110 Cal.App.4th 1273, 1283.) Here, there is no explanation how defendant will be prejudiced if the garden variety notice, indemnity, and choice of law provisions are disclosed. The motion is also not narrowly tailored. For example, it is unclear how plaintiff's mere assertion at UMF 13 that "[t]he Oracle Defendants concede that the Escrow Agreement and the Purchase Agreement are unambiguous" needs to be sealed.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling with a cover letter reflecting compliance with CRC 3.1312.
